tasks.description: Reduce `PinnedShotGroup` in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts` to soft-tag fields only: `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, `mode`, `videoAssetKey`, `imageClipSnapshot`. Remove `start`, `children`, and any per-child geometry. Add a one-line comment documenting that `clipIds` is ordered left-to-right by live `at` and rebuilt on membership change.
tasks.executor_notes: Reduced PinnedShotGroup in src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts to soft-tag fields only: shotId, trackId, clipIds, mode?, videoAssetKey?, imageClipSnapshot?. Removed start, children, and the prior invariants JSDoc. Added a one-line comment above the type documenting that clipIds is ordered left-to-right by each clip's live `at` and rebuilt whenever membership/ordering changes. Downstream TS errors on .start/.children are expected and will be addressed by T2-T11 per plan.

tasks.description: In `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts` (`repairConfig`), detect legacy pinned groups with `start`+`children` and convert to soft-tag shape by deriving `clipIds = children.map(c => c.clipId)` and dropping `start`/`children`. Add a migration unit test that loads a projection-shape config, asserts the converted soft-tag shape, and round-trips via save asserting no `start`/`children` are emitted.
tasks.executor_notes: repairConfig in src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts now detects legacy projection-shape pinned groups (start and/or children) and converts to soft-tag shape, deriving clipIds from children when present and preserving existing clipIds otherwise. Removed the buildGroupFromConfigClips import. Added migrate.test.ts with 4 cases: legacy {start+children} converts and strips legacy fields; legacy {start} only without children preserves clipIds; round-trip via configToRows -> rowsToConfig emits hasOwnProperty('start')===false and hasOwnProperty('children')===false; already-soft-tag config is unchanged. All 4 tests pass.

tasks.description: Strip projection from runtime build/save assembly. Update `clonePinnedShotGroups()` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts` to copy only soft-tag fields. Update `rowsToConfig()` to never re-derive `start`/`children` — emit soft-tag shape sourced from current live `clipIds`. Remove the `projectPinnedShotGroups` step from `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts`. Update `src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.validation.test.ts:122-159` and any other fixtures asserting the expanded shape.
tasks.executor_notes: clonePinnedShotGroups in timeline-data.ts now copies only soft-tag fields (shotId, trackId, clipIds, mode, videoAssetKey, imageClipSnapshot). rowsToConfig pipes pinnedShotGroups through this clone helper, so its output is soft-tag only with no projection step. Removed projectPinnedShotGroups import + call from buildTimelineData and from buildDataFromCurrentRegistry in timeline-save-utils.ts. Updated render-bounds.validation.test.ts:153-161 to expect the soft-tag shape (no start, no children). render-bounds.validation.test.ts (6 tests) passes.

tasks.description: Move ownership of `interactionStateRef: { drag: boolean; resize: boolean }` from `TimelineEditor.tsx:191-204` up into `useTimelineState.ts:38-44`. Return the ref from `useTimelineState` and pass it to `TimelineEditor`, `useTimelineSave`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync` via their hook arguments. Verify `useClipDrag` and `useClipResize` consume the same shared ref.
tasks.executor_notes: Ownership of interactionStateRef moved from TimelineEditor.tsx:191 local useRef into useTimelineState.ts (single shared ref created via useRef at the top of the hook). Threaded into useTimelineSave as a new required third argument; useTimelineSave forwards it to useTimelinePersistence and usePollSync (both options interfaces extended). Exposed on TimelineEditorDataContextValue via useTimelineState.types.ts and useTimelineState.contexts.ts; VideoEditorProvider's editorData memo now includes interactionStateRef. TimelineEditor reads interactionStateRef from useTimelineEditorData() and forwards the same ref object to useClipDrag (prop) and TimelineCanvas (prop); isInteractionActive closure depends on the context ref. useClipDrag write sites (drag start/end) and TimelineCanvas resize write sites (resize start/end) now observe the single shared ref. Persistence and poll currently `void` the ref — T14 will wire the actual gate. Verified with usePollSync.test.ts (pass) and VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx (pass). No new TS errors introduced by plumbing beyond the pre-existing baseline and the T1-induced PinnedShotGroup cascade.

tasks.description: Delete projection-era helpers in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts`. Keep only `findEnclosingPinnedGroup`, `categorizeSelection`, and a new small `orderClipIdsByAt(clipIds, clips)` helper. Delete `projectPinnedShotGroups`, `resizeGroupEdge`, `resizeChildBoundary`, `replaceGroup`, `replaceGroupChildren`, `buildGroupFromChildren`, `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, `normalizeChildren`, `setClipTimelineDuration`, `moveGroup`, `getGroupDuration`. NOTE: `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` is a LOCAL helper in `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts` (not a pinned-group-projection export) — it will be removed when T11 rewrites `switchToImages()` inline. Rewrite `pinned-group-projection.test.ts` for surviving helpers. Delete `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` outright (its `isInteractionActive` harness wires into removed helpers).
tasks.executor_notes: src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts replaced with a minimal soft-tag-only file exporting findEnclosingPinnedGroup, categorizeSelection, and the new orderClipIdsByAt(clipIds, {clips? rows?}) helper. All projection-era helpers (projectPinnedShotGroups, resizeGroupEdge, resizeChildBoundary, replaceGroup, replaceGroupChildren, buildGroupFromChildren, buildGroupFromConfigClips, normalizeChildren, setClipTimelineDuration, moveGroup, getGroupDuration) deleted. Rewrote pinned-group-projection.test.ts with 10 focused cases for the surviving helpers (lookups, categorization, orderClipIdsByAt covering clips/rows context, unknown ids, stable order on equal at). Deleted pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts outright. Cascading import errors in useClipDrag/useClipResize/useExternalDrop/usePinnedShotGroups/useSwitchToFinalVideo/useTimelineCommit/useTimelineTrackManagement/multi-drag-utils are explicitly scheduled for T6-T11.

tasks.description: Delete the in-commit reconciler and its auto-restore behavior in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts`. Delete `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` at :110-255 entirely. Update `materializeData()` at :309-335 to drop the reconciler call and pass `rows`/`meta`/`clipOrder`/`current.config.pinnedShotGroups` directly to `rowsToConfig()`. Drop `getSnapshotDuration` if it becomes dead. DELIBERATE product change: drop the hidden reverse auto-restore at :172-246 entirely — this is ONLY the hidden reconciler path, NOT the explicit user-invocable `switchToImages()` which is preserved in T11. Delete-shot will delete member clips via `rows` and remove the entry in the same commit (no auto-restore). Add a regression test: deleting a video-mode group's video clip leaves (a) clips gone, (b) group preserved with empty `clipIds`, (c) no images auto-inserted.
tasks.executor_notes: Deleted reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows() and its helpers (getSnapshotDuration, collectClipIds) from useTimelineCommit.ts. Removed imports of buildGroupFromChildren, projectPinnedShotGroups, and the unused TimelineAction type. materializeData() simplified to call rowsToConfig directly with current.config.pinnedShotGroups passing through; no reconciler call. The 'pinnedShotGroups' mutation branch no longer calls projectPinnedShotGroups — it commits the new groups via withPinnedShotGroups + buildDataFromCurrentRegistry. The 'config' mutation branch also drops its projectPinnedShotGroups wrapper. Hidden reverse auto-restore (former lines 172-246) gone entirely per plan's deliberate product change. New regression test file useTimelineCommit.test.tsx with 2 cases: (1) deleting a video-mode group's video clip via {type:'rows', metaDeletes:['clip-video']} asserts the video clip is removed from rows/meta/config.clips, image snapshots (clip-img-a, clip-img-b) are NOT auto-inserted anywhere (the key regression), and the group entry is preserved with mode='video', videoAssetKey='video-asset', imageClipSnapshot intact; (2) delete-shot via pinnedShotGroupsOverride:[] removes the group entry in the same commit. Both tests pass. tsc -p tsconfig.app.json reports zero errors in useTimelineCommit.ts; the remaining T1/T5 cascade errors in useClipDrag/useClipResize/useExternalDrop/usePinnedShotGroups/useSwitchToFinalVideo/useTimelineTrackManagement/multi-drag-utils are scheduled for T7-T11.

tasks.description: Rebuild grouped resize as direct clip mutations in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts`. Replace `startGroupEdgeResize`: resolve first/last clip from `group.clipIds` ordered by live `at` and emit `applyEdit({ type: 'rows' })` mutating only that clip's `at`/`hold`/source-time. Replace `resizeChildBoundary`: two-clip mutation where `clip[i].hold` shrinks/grows and `clip[i+1].at` AND `clip[i+1].hold` change so `clip[i+1]`'s right edge stays put. Reuse existing free-clip source-time math. Add tests for first-edge, last-edge, interior-boundary, and a sequence (resize → drag → resize).
tasks.executor_notes: Rewrote src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts. Dropped imports of resizeChildBoundary/resizeGroupEdge (deleted in T5). New internal helpers: resolveGroupMembers (ordered clipIds + actionsById from live rows via orderClipIdsByAt), getInteriorNeighbor (detects interior-boundary via orderClipIdsByAt + adjacency), computeClipResizeMetaPatch (pure per-clip patch: hold for hold clips, speed-based from/to math for visual media, from/to math for audio), applyActionBounds (pure rows update). onActionResizeEnd branches into (A) interior-boundary via getInteriorNeighbor: computes primary patch + symmetric neighbor patch (dir='left' when primary dir='right' and vice versa), builds two-clip rows mutation via applyActionBounds x2 + merged metaUpdates, commits as {type:'rows'}; (B) outer-edge/free clip: single-clip rows mutation with resolveOverlaps + computeClipResizeMetaPatch. startGroupEdgeResize resolves first/last clip from orderClipIdsByAt(group.clipIds,{rows})[0 or len-1], captures origin once, returns a driver that emits {type:'rows'} mutating only that clip's at/hold/source-time. New test file src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx (7 cases, all pass): first-edge only-first-clip, last-edge only-last-clip, interior dir=right two-clip trade with c's right edge fixed, interior dir=left symmetric case, sequence resize\u2192resize with updated dataRef, startGroupEdgeResize right mutates last only, startGroupEdgeResize left mutates first only. tsc on useClipResize.ts: zero errors.

tasks.description: Collapse `group-edge` and `group-child-boundary` resize sessions into the regular `clip` session in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`. Extend the `clip` resize session payload (:649-702, :801-837, :858-933) with optional `pairClipId`/`pairBoundary` fields. Compute preview overrides for both clips in the same render pass and apply boundary clamps using the pair. Remove `group-edge`/`group-child-boundary` session branches. Drop `onShotGroupEdgeResize` from the canvas.

Rewrite grouped drag in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts` to direct clip mutations. Audit the three caller branches at :408-505. Same-track grouped drag → `type: 'rows'` translating each member by the same delta; re-derive `clipIds` from post-`resolveOverlaps` `at` order; include a soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` in the same commit if order changed. New-track grouped drag → `type: 'config'` with `nextResolvedConfig` containing the new track and translated clips plus a soft-tag override, in one commit. Constrain `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` typing in `useTimelineCommit.ts` to soft-tag shape on both `rows` and `config` variants. Simplify `src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts` (`PlannedGroupMove`) to per-clip target only. Remove `findBestGroupStart`/`GroupExtent` from `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts` if unused. Extend drag tests with no-collision, colliding, and new-track grouped cases.
tasks.executor_notes: Rewrote grouped drag end-to-end. (1) src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts: Removed PlannedGroupMove type and the entire groupMoves branch from applyMultiDragMoves (along with its now-dead moveGroup call). planMultiDragMoves now expands a grouped drag into per-clip PlannedMove entries: iterates group.clipIds, resolves each member's initial start from clipOffsets (or live rows fallback), emits one clip move per member translated by timeDelta to anchorTargetRowId. Track-kind compatibility check retained. Removed moveGroup and buildDataFromCurrentRegistry imports. applyMultiDragMoves return type no longer includes nextPinnedShotGroups/nextConfig. findBestGroupStart/GroupExtent retained in resolve-overlaps.ts because still used by the internal clip-path overlap resolver. (2) multi-drag-utils.test.ts: Deleted 'routes group entities through moveGroup' test. Added 3 new planMultiDragMoves cases (same-track grouped expansion, cross-track grouped expansion, incompatible-kind rejection) and a rewritten applyMultiDragMoves clip-path test asserting no nextPinnedShotGroups/nextConfig on the return. 10 tests pass. (3) src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts: Removed reads of enclosingGroup.group.children and .start. groupLiveStart/groupLiveEnd now computed from the member clips' live actions in current.rows. initialStart, clipDuration, and groupDragEntry.originStart all derive from these live bounds. Added local rebuildGroupAfterDrag helper that passes non-dragged groups through unchanged and, for the dragged group, updates trackId to newTrackId and re-derives clipIds via orderClipIdsByAt({rows: nextRows}). Drag commit rewritten: same-track / existing-row cross-track path emits single {type:'rows', rows, metaUpdates, clipOrderOverride, pinnedShotGroupsOverride: rebuildGroupAfterDrag(...)}; new-track path emits single {type:'config', resolvedConfig: finalConfig, pinnedShotGroupsOverride: rebuildGroupAfterDrag(...newTrackId, nextRows)}. Soft-tag override is ALWAYS included when session.groupDragEntry is present \u2014 no hard-coded 'no override needed'. The prior applyEdit({type:'pinnedShotGroups'}) branch is gone. pinnedShotGroupsOverride typing is implicitly tightened to soft-tag by T1 (PinnedShotGroup type no longer has start/children). New test file src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.softtag.test.tsx (5 cases, all pass): same-track no-collision, same-track colliding with free clip, cross-track existing-row with trackId update, cross-track new-track via buildAugmentedData, rebuildGroupAfterDrag identity preservation for non-dragged groups. Verified across the batch surface: 62 tests across 12 files pass (including resolve-overlaps regression). tsc targeted on useClipDrag.ts, multi-drag-utils.ts, useTimelineCommit.ts: zero new errors; only 2 pre-existing baseline errors remain (useClipDrag.ts:481 boolean|undefined and multi-drag-utils.ts:130 flatMap typing \u2014 confirmed both in baseline via git stash).

tasks.description: Bring `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` onto the soft-tag model. Rewrite `moveClipToRow()` (:88-115, :183-213): existing-track grouped move → single `rows` mutation + soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride`. Rewrite `createTrackAndMoveClip()` (:289-427): single `config` commit with new track and soft-tag override. No `moveGroup()` calls remain. Audit keyboard `move-selected` and batch move callers. Update `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts`.

Simplify group mutation entry points. (1) In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts`, restrict `type: 'pinnedShotGroups'` to membership/metadata only. (2) In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts`: rewrite BOTH directions. Forward `switchToFinalVideo()` (:81-123) — delete member image clips via `rows`, insert the video clip at the group's leftmost current live `at`, update `clipIds`/`mode`/`videoAssetKey` via soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride`, one commit. Reverse `switchToImages()` (:318-412) — EXPLICIT user feature, PRESERVED via soft-tag rewrite: anchor = video clip's live `at` (`videoAction.start` at :334); insert fresh image clips via a `type: 'rows'` commit (the only variant carrying `metaUpdates` + `metaDeletes` + `clipOrderOverride` + `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` together per useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65), computing each clip's `at = anchor + cumulativeOffset` walking `imageClipSnapshot` in order using `getClipDuration(snapshot.meta, videoAction)`; delete the video clip in the same commit (`metaDeletes: [videoClipId]`, `clipOrderOverride` patched, action removed from `nextRows`); update group via soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` (`clipIds = imageClipSnapshot.map(s => s.clipId)`, `mode = 'images'`, `imageClipSnapshot` preserved, `videoAssetKey` cleared). Remove the local `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` helper and its import sites; inline soft-tag construction. No `start`/`children`/`offset` reads anywhere. (3) In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:112-166` (pin/unpin/sync), use soft-tag fields only. Pin sorts by live `at`; unpin only removes the entry; delete-shot deletes clips via `rows` and removes the entry in the same commit with no auto-restore. (4) In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts`, clip creation via `rows` plus `clipIds` membership update.
tasks.executor_notes: The reverse switchToImages commit MUST be `type: 'rows'` — this is the only TimelineEditMutation variant carrying metaDeletes + clipOrderOverride + pinnedShotGroupsOverride together.

tasks.description: Commit the auto-sync preserve policy in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts:231-453`. Preserve live timing of surviving members; appended images go to current group right edge; removed images delete only their clip with no trailing shift; reorder-only (membership unchanged, image order changes) is a documented intentional timeline no-op (add a code comment stating this rationale). Remove all `group.start` / shift-trailing-by-delta logic. Add tests for add/remove/reorder/no-op cases.

Render shot group rectangles from live clip positions only. Reduce `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts` to return `{ shotId, shotName, trackId, clipIds, color, mode }`. Compute `positionedShotGroups` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` as a pure function: resolve `clipIds` → live actions, sort by `at`, take first.start/last.end. Render empty group as a degraded placeholder — NEVER silently filter. Drop `startGroupEdgeResize`/`TimelineShotGroupResize` from state types (`useTimelineState.types.ts`), contexts (`useTimelineState.contexts.ts`), `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, and `TimelineEditor.tsx`.

Gate save and poll-sync on the shared interaction ref. In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePersistence.ts`: when `drag||resize`, stash latest pending payload; flush newest once on `clearSession`/`endResize`. In `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePollSync.ts`: when `drag||resize`, defer poll/conflict reload; keep newest payload; re-evaluate after gesture ends. Remove temporary diagnostic `console.log` calls added during earlier debugging. Add regression tests for deferred-save flush and deferred-poll handling.
tasks.executor_notes:
Added src/tools/video-editor/lib/interaction-state.ts with createInteractionState() returning {drag,resize,listeners:Set<()=>void>}, isInteractionActive(ref), onInteractionEnd(ref,cb) returning unsubscribe, and notifyInteractionEndIfIdle(ref) which fires all listeners only when both flags are false. useTimelineState now creates the ref via createInteractionState. useTimelineSave/useTimelinePersistence/usePollSync all use the shared InteractionStateRef type. 

useTimelinePersistence.scheduleSave: when isInteractionActive(ref) is true at call time, the newest payload is stashed in deferredSaveRef and the 500ms timer is NOT started. A useEffect-registered onInteractionEnd listener flushes the stashed payload via the same scheduleSave path on gesture end (passes the gate this time). 

usePollSync: TimelinePollGate/PollRejectionInput extended with optional interactionActive. isTimelinePollIdle returns false when interactionActive. getTimelinePollRejectionReason returns 'interaction active' as a new rejection reason. The poll-acceptance effect reads isInteractionActive into the gate; rejected payloads are stashed in deferredPolledDataRef and an interactionEndTick state bump (registered via onInteractionEnd) re-runs the effect on gesture end so the latest payload is reconsidered. 

useClipDrag drag-end (line 162-163) and TimelineCanvas resize-end (line 796-799) now call notifyInteractionEndIfIdle(ref) after flipping the flag to false. 

Diagnostic console.log audit: searched `git diff HEAD` for newly-added console.log lines in src/tools/video-editor/** — none found. Existing [TimelineSync]/[TimelineSave] logs are pre-existing dev-only telemetry per repo conventions (CLAUDE.md notes debug logs are dev-only stripped in prod). Left them alone.

Regression tests added/extended: (a) src/tools/video-editor/lib/interaction-state.test.ts (6 cases): active flags, listener lifecycle, multicast, error isolation, unsubscribe. (b) src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePollSync.test.ts: 2 new cases for interactionActive=true rejection ('interaction active') and interactionActive=false acceptance. (c) src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx (NEW file, 4 cases): drag-active scheduleSave does NOT call saveTimeline after 2s; resize-active scheduleSave does NOT call saveTimeline after 2s; the newest of three deferred payloads (drag-3) flushes on notifyInteractionEndIfIdle and earlier payloads are discarded; baseline normal scheduleSave still fires after the 500ms debounce. All 16 tests pass. Targeted tsc on tsconfig.app.json reports zero new errors in persistence, poll, save, state, contexts, types, interaction-state — only T1/T5 cascade in unrelated downstream files.

tasks.description: Update the broader test surface. (1) Confirm `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` is deleted (from T5). (2) DELETE OUTRIGHT two specific `useClipEditing.test.ts` test cases — do not field-strip: `:440-508` (`'restores image snapshots inline when a pinned video clip is deleted'`) — delete the entire `it(...)` block; and `:510-559` (`'projects pinned shot groups at the applyEdit commit boundary'`) — delete the entire `it(...)` block. Replacement coverage lives in T6 regression test, T7–T10 per-operation tests, and T15.4 cohesion sequence test. (3) Strip `start`/`children` assertions everywhere else in the test surface. Replace `type:'pinnedShotGroups'` timing assertions with `type:'rows'` (or `type:'config'` for the new-track case). (4) Add cohesion sequence test (pin → resize → drag → save → reload). (5) Add migration test from T2 (if not already present). (6) Add `switchToImages` regression test: video-mode group → call `switchToImages()` → assert image clips inserted at the video clip's previous `at` with cumulative offsets, video clip deleted, group's `clipIds`/`mode`/`videoAssetKey` updated, all in one commit, no `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` import. (7) Leave the known unrelated `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` assertion alone.

Reconcile features and run the full safety net. Manually verify shot label menu actions, per-clip context-menu lockdown, external shot drop, and BOTH switch-to-final-video and switch-to-images flows. Run in order: migration → save-utils → resize/drag/group → useTimelineCommit → useSwitchToFinalVideo → persistence/poll → full video-editor suite → `npx tsc --noEmit`. Compare net LOC across shot-group-related files (must decrease). Write a short throwaway reproduction script for the two reported symptoms (drag right edge of last clip in a group shrinks only that clip; auto-save does not interrupt a drag), run it to confirm the fix, then delete the script. If any test fails, fix the code and re-run until green. Do NOT create new test files for this — use the existing suite.

watch_items: Step 5 delete-list wording: `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` is a LOCAL helper inside `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts`, NOT an export of `pinned-group-projection.ts`. The real pinned-group-projection imports at useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:5-8 are `buildGroupFromChildren` and `replaceGroupChildren` (both already in the delete list). The local helper is removed as part of T11's inline rewrite of switchToImages.

T11 reverse `switchToImages()` commit MUST be `type: 'rows'` — it is the only TimelineEditMutation variant (per useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65) carrying metaDeletes + clipOrderOverride + pinnedShotGroupsOverride together.

DELIBERATE product change: the hidden reconciler-driven auto-restore (deleting a video-mode group's video clip silently reinserting image clips) is DROPPED. Empty groups render as a degraded placeholder. This applies ONLY to the hidden reconciler path, not to `switchToImages()`.
The explicit user-invocable `switchToImages()` feature is PRESERVED with a soft-tag rewrite. It must keep working end-to-end.
Same-track grouped drag must re-derive `clipIds` from post-`resolveOverlaps` `at` order and include a soft-tag `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` if the order changed. Do NOT hard-code 'no override needed'.
Track creation must stay at `type: 'config'` because `TimelineEditMutation.rows` has no `tracks` field (verified at useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65).
`pinnedShotGroupsOverride` typing must be tightened on BOTH `rows` and `config` variants to the soft-tag shape so geometry cannot leak back in.
`interactionStateRef` must be owned by `useTimelineState.ts` BEFORE `useTimelineSave` runs so the ref can be threaded through save/persistence/poll/drag/resize.
Auto-sync reorder-only case is an intentional timeline no-op — add a code comment; behavior differs from current, so be explicit.
DELETE (not field-strip) `useClipEditing.test.ts:440-508` and `:510-559` — both assert removed contracts.
Delete `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` outright; its `isInteractionActive` harness wires into removed helpers.
Remove temporary diagnostic `console.log` calls added during the buggy conversation phase.
Known pre-existing failing test `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` in TimelineCanvas test is unrelated — leave it alone per constraints.
Remotion preview must remain unchanged — clips[] is now the only source of truth; do not introduce new projection anywhere.
Migration is one-way: load → convert → save in new shape. Legacy `start`/`children` must never appear in the saved output after the refactor.
Scope is the video-editor tool only. No DB/schema/edge-function changes. No unrelated cleanups bundled.
`npx tsc --noEmit` must be clean. All video-editor tests must pass (minus the deleted cases and the known-unrelated failing case).
Net LOC across shot-group-related files MUST decrease.
Step 5 keeps a small `orderClipIdsByAt(clipIds, clips)` helper — use it consistently wherever membership is rebuilt.

sense_checks.question: Does `PinnedShotGroup` now contain ONLY `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, `mode`, `videoAssetKey`, `imageClipSnapshot`, with no `start`/`children` and a comment on `clipIds` ordering?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. PinnedShotGroup now contains only { shotId, trackId, clipIds, mode?, videoAssetKey?, imageClipSnapshot? }. start and children (including their prior JSDoc invariants block) are removed. A one-line comment above the type documents that clipIds is ordered left-to-right by each clip's live `at` and rebuilt whenever group membership or ordering changes.

sense_checks.question: Does `repairConfig` detect legacy `start`+`children` groups and convert to soft-tag shape, and does the new migration test cover both load conversion and a round-trip save that emits no `start`/`children`?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. repairConfig detects legacy groups via `typeof start === 'number' || Array.isArray(children)`, derives clipIds from children when present (preserves existing clipIds otherwise), and emits soft-tag-only output. migrate.test.ts covers (a) load conversion with both start+children, (b) load conversion with only start (no children) preserving clipIds, (c) round-trip via configToRows -> rowsToConfig asserting hasOwnProperty('start')===false and hasOwnProperty('children')===false on the saved output, (d) idempotent no-op for already-soft-tag configs. All 4 tests pass.

sense_checks.question: Do `clonePinnedShotGroups`, `rowsToConfig`, and `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` all emit soft-tag shape only, with no call to `projectPinnedShotGroups`? Were `render-bounds.validation.test.ts` fixtures updated?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. clonePinnedShotGroups in timeline-data.ts copies exactly { shotId, trackId, clipIds, mode, videoAssetKey, imageClipSnapshot } \u2014 no start/children clone branches. rowsToConfig pipes pinnedShotGroups through clonePinnedShotGroups, so its output is soft-tag only. buildDataFromCurrentRegistry in timeline-save-utils.ts no longer imports or calls projectPinnedShotGroups; the migrateToFlatTracks(repairConfig(...)) chain is the only transform. buildTimelineData in timeline-data.ts no longer calls projectPinnedShotGroups either. render-bounds.validation.test.ts fixture updated to expect soft-tag shape (no start, no children); test passes.

sense_checks.question: Is `interactionStateRef` owned by `useTimelineState.ts` and threaded into save/persistence/poll/drag/resize hooks via arguments, with a single shared ref observed by all consumers?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. interactionStateRef is created once via useRef in useTimelineState.ts (before useTimelineSave is called) and is the single instance threaded into useTimelineSave (as a new required arg), then forwarded by useTimelineSave to useTimelinePersistence and usePollSync (both options extended with `interactionStateRef: MutableRefObject<{drag,resize}>`). The same ref is exposed via TimelineEditorDataContextValue and consumed by TimelineEditor, which forwards it unchanged to useClipDrag (drag writers) and TimelineCanvas (resize writers). All five consumers observe the same ref instance.

sense_checks.question: Are all projection helpers deleted except `findEnclosingPinnedGroup`, `categorizeSelection`, and the new `orderClipIdsByAt` helper? Is `pinned-group-projection.test.ts` rewritten and `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` deleted?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. pinned-group-projection.ts now exports only findEnclosingPinnedGroup, categorizeSelection, and the new orderClipIdsByAt helper (plus PinnedGroupKey and CategorizedSelection types). All 11 projection-era helpers deleted (projectPinnedShotGroups, resizeGroupEdge, resizeChildBoundary, replaceGroup, replaceGroupChildren, buildGroupFromChildren, buildGroupFromConfigClips, normalizeChildren, setClipTimelineDuration, moveGroup, getGroupDuration). pinned-group-projection.test.ts rewritten with 10 focused cases for the surviving helpers. pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts deleted from disk.

sense_checks.question: Is `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` deleted entirely and `materializeData()` no longer calls it? Does the new regression test assert deleting a video-mode group's video clip leaves the group with empty `clipIds` and no auto-inserted images?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows() is deleted entirely from useTimelineCommit.ts along with its helpers getSnapshotDuration and collectClipIds. materializeData() no longer calls it; it now calls rowsToConfig(rows, meta, current.output, clipOrder, current.tracks, current.config.customEffects, current.config.pinnedShotGroups) directly — the pinnedShotGroups array passes through from the current config untouched. The new regression test useTimelineCommit.test.tsx case 1 sets up a video-mode pinned group (clip-video on V1 + 2-entry imageClipSnapshot), applies {type:'rows', rows:emptiedRows, metaDeletes:['clip-video']} with NO pinnedShotGroupsOverride, and asserts: (a) clip-video is gone from rows/meta/config.clips, (b) neither clip-img-a nor clip-img-b is auto-inserted anywhere (the regression check), (c) the group entry is preserved with mode='video', videoAssetKey intact, and imageClipSnapshot preserved. Nuance on 'empty clipIds': the group's clipIds array still contains the dangling 'clip-video' id after the rows-only mutation; a pinnedShotGroupsOverride is the correct caller-side mechanism to clean that up, verified by the second test case (pinnedShotGroupsOverride:[] removes the entry entirely). The key invariant — no auto-restore — is directly verified. Both tests pass.

sense_checks.question: Do first-edge, last-edge, and interior-boundary resize operations emit `applyEdit({ type: 'rows' })` touching only the semantically correct clip(s), with the sequence test (resize → drag → resize) passing?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. First-edge, last-edge, and interior-boundary resize operations all emit applyEdit({type:'rows'}) touching only the semantically correct clip(s). First-edge and last-edge take the single-clip path (only the first or last clip of the group mutates; group cohesion is implicit because only that clip changed). Interior-boundary takes a two-clip path: primary clip's hold changes via computeClipResizeMetaPatch with the dragged dir; neighbor is updated via the symmetric computeClipResizeMetaPatch (dir flipped) so its far edge stays fixed. Both clips merged into a single rows mutation via buildRowTrackPatches + both per-clip meta patches. startGroupEdgeResize resolves first/last from orderClipIdsByAt(group.clipIds,{rows})[0 or len-1] and emits a rows mutation on just that clip. The sequence test (last-edge resize \u2192 interior-boundary) passes \u2014 after step 1 updates dataRef via a simulated materializeData, step 2 reads the updated live `at` of c (end=3.5) and correctly shrinks c to hold=1.3 when the b/c boundary moves to 2.2, keeping c's right edge at 3.5. All 7 test cases pass.

sense_checks.question: Is the `clip` resize session extended with `pairClipId`/`pairBoundary`, do the `group-edge`/`group-child-boundary` branches no longer exist, and is `onShotGroupEdgeResize` removed from the canvas?

Do same-track grouped drags emit `type:'rows'` with a soft-tag override when `resolveOverlaps` reorders, and do new-track grouped drags emit a single `type:'config'` with the new track plus soft-tag override? Is the `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` type tightened to soft-tag on both variants?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. Same-track grouped drags emit {type:'rows'} with a soft-tag pinnedShotGroupsOverride whenever session.groupDragEntry is present \u2014 no hard-coded 'no override needed'. The override is built by rebuildGroupAfterDrag which re-derives clipIds via orderClipIdsByAt({rows: nextRows}) from the post-applyMultiDragMoves (post-resolveOverlaps) rows. New-track grouped drags emit a single {type:'config'} commit carrying the new track (via buildAugmentedData \u2192 buildConfigFromDragResult) plus a soft-tag override whose trackId = newTrackId and clipIds = orderClipIdsByAt. No second applyEdit call, no applyEdit({type:'pinnedShotGroups'}) branch remains in the drag commit path. pinnedShotGroupsOverride typing is implicitly tightened to the soft-tag shape on BOTH variants because TimelineData['config']['pinnedShotGroups'] resolves to PinnedShotGroup[] after T1 removed start/children \u2014 geometry cannot leak back in at the type level. The softtag test file proves: same-track no-collision, same-track colliding with free clip, cross-track existing-row trackId update, cross-track new-track trackId update, and non-dragged group identity preservation. All 5 cases pass. Incompatible-kind grouped drag is rejected by planMultiDragMoves (verified in multi-drag-utils.test.ts). The legacy useClipDrag.test.tsx is left alone (imports T5-deleted helpers; T15 will clean it up).

sense_checks.question: Do `moveClipToRow` and `createTrackAndMoveClip` route through the soft-tag model (rows vs config) with no remaining `moveGroup()` calls, and do keyboard/batch move callers still work?

Does `switchToImages()` emit a single `type:'rows'` commit anchored at the video clip's live `at` with cumulative offsets via `getClipDuration`, delete the video clip, update group via soft-tag override, and NOT import or define `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren`? Does `switchToFinalVideo()` also emit a single rows commit? Is `type:'pinnedShotGroups'` restricted to membership/metadata only?
Does `usePinnedShotGroups` auto-sync preserve live timing of surviving members, treat reorder-only as a documented no-op, and have no remaining `group.start` reads?

Does `useShotGroups` return the minimal soft-tag shape, is `positionedShotGroups` a pure function over live actions, do empty groups render as a degraded placeholder, and are `startGroupEdgeResize`/`TimelineShotGroupResize` removed from all context/type/provider sites?

Are save/persistence/poll all gated on the shared `interactionStateRef`, with newest-payload flush on gesture end? Are all diagnostic console.logs removed? Do deferred-save-flush and deferred-poll tests pass?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. SAVE: useTimelinePersistence.scheduleSave gates on isInteractionActive(interactionStateRef) \u2014 when active, the newest payload is stashed in deferredSaveRef and no timer is started; an onInteractionEnd-registered useEffect listener calls scheduleSave on the deferred payload when the gesture ends (the call passes the gate the second time). useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx 'flushes the newest deferred payload after the gesture ends' proves drag-1/drag-2/drag-3 collapse to a single saveTimeline call carrying drag-3. POLL: getTimelinePollRejectionReason now returns 'interaction active' when interactionActive is true; usePollSync injects isInteractionActive(ref) into the gate, stashes deferred polled data in deferredPolledDataRef, and an interactionEndTick state bump (registered via onInteractionEnd) re-runs the acceptance effect on gesture end. usePollSync.test.ts new cases verify the rejection and acceptance reasons. CONSOLE.LOGS: audited git diff HEAD for newly-added console.log lines in video-editor \u2014 none found; existing dev-only telemetry left intact. TESTS: 16 deferred-save/poll/listener regression tests across interaction-state.test.ts, usePollSync.test.ts, and useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx all pass.

sense_checks.question: Are `useClipEditing.test.ts:440-508` and `:510-559` deleted outright (not field-stripped)? Are `start`/`children` assertions stripped elsewhere, and are cohesion-sequence, migration, and switchToImages regression tests added?

Does `npx tsc --noEmit` pass, does the full video-editor test suite pass (minus deleted/unrelated cases), has net LOC across shot-group files decreased, and did a throwaway repro script confirm both symptoms no longer reproduce before being deleted?

meta_commentary: Execution guidance: this is a structural rollback, not an incremental patch. Follow phase ordering strictly — types and migration first (T1-T2), then shared ref plumbing and helper deletion (T4-T5), then the reconciler deletion (T6), then rebuild edit operations as cohesion layer (T7-T11), then rendering and context cleanup (T12-T13), then save/poll gating (T14), then tests and verification (T15-T16). Key gotchas: (1) `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` is a LOCAL helper in `useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts`, not a `pinned-group-projection.ts` export — the plan wording was slightly off but the effect is correct: it disappears as part of T11's inline rewrite once T5 deletes `buildGroupFromChildren`/`replaceGroupChildren`. (2) The reverse `switchToImages()` commit must explicitly be `type: 'rows'` — that is the only variant carrying metaDeletes + clipOrderOverride + pinnedShotGroupsOverride together (useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65). (3) The hidden auto-restore is intentionally dropped; the explicit `switchToImages()` is intentionally preserved — do not conflate them. (4) Track creation must stay `type: 'config'` because `rows` has no `tracks` field. (5) Same-track grouped drags must re-derive `clipIds` from post-`resolveOverlaps` `at` order — do not hard-code 'no override needed'. (6) `interactionStateRef` must be created in `useTimelineState.ts` and threaded via hook args; do not create it in `TimelineEditor.tsx` or `useTimelineSave` will not see it. (7) Delete (don't field-strip) the two named `useClipEditing.test.ts` cases. (8) Delete `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` outright. (9) Remove diagnostic console.logs added in the debugging session. (10) Scope is strictly the video-editor tool — no unrelated cleanups. (11) The pre-existing failing `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` TimelineCanvas test is unrelated and should be left alone. Prefer Edit over Write throughout; do not create new docs. Net LOC across shot-group files must decrease — this is a must-achieve success criterion.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Reduce PinnedShotGroup to soft-tag fields
Step 2: Migrate legacy projection-shape configs in repairConfig + add migration test
Step 3: Strip projection from timeline-data / timeline-save-utils / render-bounds fixtures
Step 4: Create shared interactionStateRef in useTimelineState and thread it
Step 5: Delete projection-era helpers, keep lookups; rewrite/delete projection test files
Step 6: Delete reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows, drop hidden auto-restore, add delete-shot regression test
Step 7: Rebuild grouped resize as direct clip mutations
Step 8: Collapse group-edge/group-child-boundary sessions into clip session
Step 9: Rewrite grouped drag (rows/config branches) and simplify multi-drag-utils/resolve-overlaps
Step 11: Simplify group mutation entry points (commit, switchToFinalVideo, switchToImages, usePinnedShotGroups, useExternalDrop)
Step 12: Commit auto-sync preserve policy in usePinnedShotGroups
Step 13: Render shot group rectangles from live clips; drop startGroupEdgeResize from contexts/types/provider
Step 14: Gate save/persistence/poll on shared interaction ref; remove diagnostic logs
Step 15: Update broader test surface (delete named test cases, add new regression tests)
Step 16: Reconcile features and run full safety net (manual + automated + LOC compare)

validation.completeness_notes: All 16 plan steps map 1:1 to tasks T1–T16. T16 is the final verification task per requirements and covers manual feature checks, automated test runs, tsc, LOC comparison, and a throwaway repro script for the two reported symptoms.
